sourceMovie.title: Lady Vengeance
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Lady Vengeance works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Oldboy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lady Vengeance clicked for you, Oldboy (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Faster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lady Vengeance clicked for you, Faster (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lady Vengeance?
faq.answer: Try Oldboy, Faster, Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance—each shares DNA with Lady Vengeance while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lady Vengeance?
faq.answer: Lady Vengeance (2005) is best described as Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lady Vengeance worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
